response.justification: Sarah Smith's 16-year career is built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, and web/mobile technologies with negligible production C++ exposure — roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 writing component-level C and C++, plus a bachelor thesis project. The role mandates 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows; no Windows development appears anywhere in the resume. SQL experience is limited to MySQL and generic SQL mentions, with no SQL Server or Oracle. The sole financial-services touch (RIDE Capital, 2020–2021) used Node.js and GraphQL rather than C++ and involved investment-platform UX work, not capital-markets or fixed-income systems. The candidate does not meet the mandatory technical requirements.
strengths: CI/CD and DevOps fundamentals (GitLab, Docker, Kubernetes, Artifactory) align with desirable pipeline knowledge
Java background from multiple roles (Altom, NI) partially satisfies the optional Java desirable skill
Demonstrated engineering discipline through TDD, trunk-based development, and pairing practices across several positions
concerns: C++ experience is effectively absent at production scale — under six months total with no Windows platform work
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work centres on MongoDB and MySQL
No capital-markets, fixed-income, or investment-banking domain knowledge paired with the required C++ stack

key_factors.factor: C++ depth and Windows platform absence
